title: Add record deduplication and common-tag intersection utilities
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+from typing import Any
+
+
+def deduplicate_records(records: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    """Return records with duplicates removed, preserving first-seen order."""
+    unique: list[dict] = []
+    for record in records:
+        if record not in unique:
+            unique.append(record)
+    return unique
+
+
+def common_tags(posts: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
+    """Return tags present in every post, sorted alphabetically."""
+    if not posts:
+        return []
+    shared = set(posts[0].get("tags", []))
+    for post in posts[1:]:
+        shared &= set(post.get("tags", []))
+    result: list[str] = []
+    for tag in sorted(shared):
+        if tag not in result:
+            result.append(tag)
+    return result

+
+

expected_findings.title: O(n²) duplicate check using list membership instead of a set
expected_findings.rationale: Each loop iteration scans the entire unique list to test membership, giving O(n) per check and O(n²) total. A set (or dict keyed by a hashable representation) reduces this to O(n).

expected_findings.title: Redundant O(n²) list scan after a set intersection already guarantees uniqueness
expected_findings.rationale: After the set intersection, iterating sorted(shared) yields unique elements by construction. The 'if tag not in result' membership test is always True yet performs O(n²) comparisons; the correct code is simply 'return sorted(shared)'.
